contentBlocks.original:
नमो नमो दुर्गे सुख करनी।
नमो नमो दुर्गे दुःख हरनी॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namo namo durge sukha karanī.
namo namo durge duḥkha haranī..1..
contentBlocks.translation: Opening doha and verse 1: Salutation, salutation to Durgā who creates joy! Salutation, salutation to Durgā who removes sorrow!
contentBlocks.meanings: नमो (namo) – salutations; नमो (namo) – salutations; दुर्गे (durge) – O Durga; सुख (sukha) – happiness; करनी। (karanī) – causing; नमो (namo) – salutations; नमो (namo) – salutations; दुर्गे (durge) – O Durga; दुःख (duḥkha) – sorrow; हरनी॥१॥ (haranī) – removing;

contentBlocks.original:
निरंकार है ज्योति तुम्हारी।
तिहूँ लोक फैली उजियारी॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
niraṃkāra hai jyoti tumhārī.
tihūm̐ loka phailī ujiyārī..2..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 2: Your light is formless (nirākāra). Its radiance spreads through all three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: निरंकार (niraṅkāra) – formless; है (hai) – is; ज्योति (jyoti) – light; तुम्हारी। (tumhārī) – your; तिहूँ (tihūṁ) – three; लोक (loka) – worlds; फैली (phailī) – spread; उजियारी॥२॥ (ujiyārī) – brightness;

contentBlocks.original:
शशि ललाट मुख महाविशाला।
नेत्र लाल भृकुटि विकराला॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaśi lalāṭa mukha mahāviśālā.
netra lāla bhṛkuṭi vikarālā..3..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 3: You have a moon-marked forehead and a face most vast. Your eyes are crimson and your eyebrows terrifying.
contentBlocks.meanings: शशि (śaśi) – moon; ललाट (lalāṭa) – forehead; मुख (mukha) – face; महाविशाला। (mahāviśālā) – very large; नेत्र (netra) – eye; लाल (lāla) – red; भृकुटि (bhṛkuṭi) – brow; विकराला॥३॥ (vikarālā) – fierce;

contentBlocks.original:
रूप मातु को अधिक सुहावे।
दरश करत जन अति सुख पावे॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rūpa mātu ko adhika suhāve.
daraśa karata jana ati sukha pāve..4..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 4: The form of the Mother is most beautiful to behold. Those who take your darśan receive immense joy.
contentBlocks.meanings: रूप (rūpa) – form; मातु (mātu) – mother; को (ko) – who; अधिक (adhika) – more; सुहावे। (suhāve) – pleasing; दरश (daraśa) – sight; करत (karat) – doing; जन (jana) – people; अति (ati) – very; सुख (sukha) – happiness; पावे॥४॥ (pāve) – attain;

contentBlocks.original:
तुम संसार शक्ति लै कीना।
पालन हेतु अन्न धन दीना॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tuma saṃsāra śakti lai kīnā.
pālana hetu anna dhana dīnā..5..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 5: You took on the śakti (divine power) of the entire universe. For its sustenance you gave food and wealth.
contentBlocks.meanings: तुम (tum) – you; संसार (saṁsāra) – world; शक्ति (śakti) – power; लै (lai) – taking; कीना। (kīnā) – did; पालन (pālana) – nurturing; हेतु (hetu) – for the sake of; अन्न (anna) – food; धन (dhana) – wealth; दीना॥५॥ (dīnā) – given;

contentBlocks.original:
अन्नपूर्णा हुई जग पाला।
तुम ही आदि सुन्दरी बाला॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
annapūrṇā huī jaga pālā.
tuma hī ādi sundarī bālā..6..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 6: As Annapūrṇā you nourish all the world. You yourself are the primordial beauty, the eternally youthful one.
contentBlocks.meanings: अन्नपूर्णा (annapūrṇā) – goddess of food; हुई (huī) – became; जग (jaga) – world; पाला। (pālā) – nurturer; तुम (tum) – you; ही (hī) – indeed; आदि (ādi) – primordial; सुन्दरी (sundarī) – beautiful; बाला॥६॥ (bālā) – young girl;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रलयकाल सब नाशन हारी।
तुम गौरी शिवशंकर प्यारी॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pralayakāla saba nāśana hārī.
tuma gaurī śivaśaṃkara pyārī..7..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 7: At the time of cosmic dissolution you destroy everything. You are Gaurī — the beloved of Śiva-Śaṅkara.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रलयकाल (pralayakāla) – time of dissolution (प्रलय:dissolution | काल:time); सब (saba) – all; नाशन (nāśana) – destroyer; हारी। (hārī) – remover; तुम (tum) – you; गौरी (gaurī) – Gauri; शिवशंकर (śivaśaṅkara) – Shiva Shankar (शिव:Shiva | शंकर:Shankar); प्यारी॥७॥ (pyārī) – beloved;

contentBlocks.original:
शिव योगी तुम्हरे गुण गावें।
ब्रह्मा विष्णु तुम्हें नित ध्यावें॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śiva yogī tumhare guṇa gāveṃ.
brahmā viṣṇu tumheṃ nita dhyāveṃ..8..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 8: The yogi Śiva sings your glories ceaselessly. Brahmā and Viṣṇu meditate upon you daily.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिव (śiva) – Shiva; योगी (yogī) – yogis; तुम्हरे (tumhare) – your; गुण (guṇa) – qualities; गावें। (gāve) – sing; ब्रह्मा (brahmā) – Brahma; विष्णु (viṣṇu) – Vishnu; तुम्हें (tumheṁ) – you; नित (nita) – constantly; ध्यावें॥८॥ (dhyāveṁ) – meditate;

contentBlocks.original:
रूप सरस्वती को तुम धारा।
दे सुबुद्धि ऋषि मुनिन उबारा॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rūpa sarasvatī ko tuma dhārā.
de subuddhi ṛṣi munina ubārā..9..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 9: You took the form of Sarasvatī. Granting right understanding, you uplifted the sages and seers.
contentBlocks.meanings: रूप (rūpa) – form; सरस्वती (sarasvatī) – Saraswati; को (ko) – to; तुम (tum) – you; धारा। (dhārā) – stream; दे (de) – give; सुबुद्धि (subuddhi) – good intellect; ऋषि (ṛṣi) – sage; मुनिन (munin) – ascetics; उबारा॥९॥ (ubārā) – uplifted;

contentBlocks.original:
धरयो रूप नरसिंह को अम्बा।
परगट भई फाड़कर खम्बा॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dharayo rūpa narasiṃha ko ambā.
paragaṭa bhaī phār̤akara khambā..10..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 10: You assumed the Narasiṃha form, O Ambā. You appeared, bursting forth from the pillar.
contentBlocks.meanings: धरयो (dharaḥ) – held; रूप (rūpa) – form; नरसिंह (narasiṁha) – Narasimha; को (ko) – who; अम्बा। (ambā) – mother; परगट (paragaṭa) – manifested; भई (bhaī) – became; फाड़कर (phāḍkara) – tearing; खम्बा॥१०॥ (khambā) – pillar;

contentBlocks.original:
रक्षा करि प्रह्लाद बचायो।
हिरण्याक्ष को स्वर्ग पठायो॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rakṣā kari prahlāda bacāyo.
hiraṇyākṣa ko svarga paṭhāyo..11..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 11: You protected and saved Prahlāda. Hiraṇyākṣa you dispatched to the other world.
contentBlocks.meanings: रक्षा (rakṣā) – protection; करि (kari) – do; प्रह्लाद (prahlāda) – Prahlada; बचायो। (bacāyo) – saved; हिरण्याक्ष (hiraṇyākṣa) – Hiranyaksha; को (ko) – to; स्वर्ग (svarga) – heaven; पठायो॥११॥ (paṭhāyo) – sent;

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मी रूप धरो जग माहीं।
श्री नारायण अंग समाहीं॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmī rūpa dharo jaga māhīṃ.
śrī nārāyaṇa aṃga samāhīṃ..12..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 12: You assumed the form of Lakṣmī in the world. You are enshrined in the very being of Śrī Nārāyaṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: लक्ष्मी (lakṣmī) – goddess of wealth; रूप (rūpa) – form; धरो (dharo) – bearer; जग (jaga) – world; माहीं। (māhīṁ) – in; श्री (śrī) – auspicious; नारायण (nārāyaṇa) – Narayana; अंग (aṅga) – limb; समाहीं॥१२॥ (samāhīṁ) – absorbed;

contentBlocks.original:
क्षीरसिन्धु में करत विलासा।
दयासिन्धु दीजै मन आसा॥१३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣīrasindhu meṃ karata vilāsā.
dayāsindhu dījai mana āsā..13..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 13: You sport in the Milky Ocean. O ocean of compassion, grant hope to my heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: क्षीरसिन्धु (kṣīrasindhu) – ocean of milk (क्षीर:milk | सिन्धु:ocean); में (meṁ) – in; करत (karata) – doing; विलासा। (vilāsā) – sporting; दयासिन्धु (dayāsindhu) – ocean of compassion (दया:compassion | सिन्धु:ocean); दीजै (dījai) – give; मन (mana) – mind; आसा॥१३॥ (āsā) – hope;

contentBlocks.original:
हिंगलाज में तुम्हीं भवानी।
महिमा अमित न जात बखानी॥१४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hiṃgalāja meṃ tumhīṃ bhavānī.
mahimā amita na jāta bakhānī..14..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 14: In Hinglaj you yourself are Bhavānī. Your glory is limitless and cannot be fully told.
contentBlocks.meanings: हिंगलाज (hiṅgalāja) – Hinglaj; में (meṃ) – in; तुम्हीं (tumhīṃ) – you alone; भवानी। (bhavānī) – Bhavani; महिमा (mahimā) – glory; अमित (amita) – immeasurable; न (na) – not; जात (jāta) – can be; बखानी॥१४॥ (bakhānī) – described;

contentBlocks.original:
मातंगी अरु धूमावति माता।
भुवनेश्वरी बगला सुख दाता॥१५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātaṃgī aru dhūmāvati mātā.
bhuvaneśvarī bagalā sukha dātā..15..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 15: You are Mātaṅgī and Mother Dhūmāvatī; Bhuvanēśvarī and Bagalā, bestowers of happiness.
contentBlocks.meanings: मातंगी (mātaṅgī) – Mātangī (goddess); अरु (aru) – null; धूमावति (dhūmāvatī) – Dhūmāvatī (goddess); माता। (mātā) – mother; भुवनेश्वरी (bhuvaneśvarī) – Bhuvaneśvarī (goddess); बगला (bagalā) – Bagalā (goddess); सुख (sukha) – happiness; दाता॥१५॥ (dātā) – giver;

contentBlocks.original:
श्री भैरव तारा जग तारिणी।
छिन्न भाल भव दुःख निवारिणी॥१६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī bhairava tārā jaga tāriṇī.
chinna bhāla bhava duḥkha nivāriṇī..16..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 16: Śrī Bhairavī, Tārā — you carry all the world across; Chinnamastā — you remove the sorrows of worldly existence.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; भैरव (bhairava) – Bhairava; तारा (tārā) – Tara; जग (jaga) – world; तारिणी। (tāriṇī) – savior; छिन्न (chinna) – cut off; भाल (bhāla) – forehead; भव (bhava) – existence; दुःख (duḥkha) – suffering; निवारिणी॥१६॥ (nivāriṇī) – remover;

contentBlocks.original:
केहरि वाहन सोह भवानी।
लांगुर वीर चलत अगवानी॥१७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kehari vāhana soha bhavānī.
lāṃgura vīra calata agavānī..17..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 17: Mounted on the lion you shine gloriously, O Bhavānī. The heroic Laṅgūra attendants march before you.
contentBlocks.meanings: केहरि (kehari) – lion; वाहन (vāhana) – vehicle; सोह (soha) – adorned; भवानी। (bhavānī) – Goddess Bhavani; लांगुर (lāṅgura) – tail; वीर (vīra) – hero; चलत (calata) – moving; अगवानी॥१७॥ (agavānī) – leading;

contentBlocks.original:
कर में खप्पर खड्ग विराजै।
जाको देख काल डर भाजै॥१८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kara meṃ khappara khaḍga virājai.
jāko dekha kāla ḍara bhājai..18..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 18: A skull-cup and sword grace your hands. At the sight of you, even Death (Kāla) flees in terror.
contentBlocks.meanings: कर (kara) – hand; में (meṃ) – in; खप्पर (khappara) – skull; खड्ग (khaḍga) – sword; विराजै। (virājai) – shines; जाको (jāko) – whose; देख (dekha) – seeing; काल (kāla) – death; डर (ḍara) – fear; भाजै॥१८॥ (bhājai) – flees;

contentBlocks.original:
सोहै अस्त्र और त्रिशूला।
जाते उठत शत्रु हिय शूला॥१९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sohai astra aura triśūlā.
jāte uṭhata śatru hiya śūlā..19..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 19: Your weapons and trident gleam with brilliance. At their sight, a sharp pain pierces the enemy's heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: सोहै (sohai) – shines; अस्त्र (astra) – weapon; और (aura) – and; त्रिशूला। (triśūlā) – trident; जाते (jāte) – go; उठत (uṭhata) – rise; शत्रु (śatru) – enemy; हिय (hiya) – heart; शूला॥१९॥ (śūlā) – pain;

contentBlocks.original:
नगरकोट में तुम्हीं विराजत।
तिहुँलोक में डंका बाजत॥२०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nagarakoṭa meṃ tumhīṃ virājata.
tihum̐loka meṃ ḍaṃkā bājata..20..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 20: In Nāgarkoṭa (Kangra) you reign supreme. Your glory resounds like a great drum through all three worlds.
contentBlocks.meanings: नगरकोट (nagarakoṭa) – Nagarkot; में (meṃ) – in; तुम्हीं (tumhīṃ) – you alone; विराजत। (virājata) – reside; तिहुँलोक (tihuṃloka) – three worlds (तिहुँ:three | लोक:worlds); में (meṃ) – in; डंका (ḍaṅkā) – drum; बाजत॥२०॥ (bājata) – resounds;

contentBlocks.original:
शुम्भ निशुम्भ दानव तुम मारे।
रक्तबीज शंखन संहारे॥२१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śumbha niśumbha dānava tuma māre.
raktabīja śaṃkhana saṃhāre..21..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 21: You slew the demons Śumbha and Niśumbha. Raktabīja and his entire army you utterly destroyed.
contentBlocks.meanings: शुम्भ (śumbha) – demon Shumbha; निशुम्भ (niśumbha) – demon Nishumbha; दानव (dānava) – demon; तुम (tum) – you; मारे। (māre) – killed; रक्तबीज (raktabīja) – Raktabija (रक्त:blood | बीज:seed); शंखन (śaṅkhana) – null; संहारे॥२१॥ (saṃhāre) – destroyed;

contentBlocks.original:
महिषासुर नृप अति अभिमानी।
जेहि अघ भार मही अकुलानी॥२२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahiṣāsura nṛpa ati abhimānī.
jehi agha bhāra mahī akulānī..22..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 22: The demon-king Mahiṣāsura was exceedingly arrogant. The weight of his sins tormented the earth.
contentBlocks.meanings: महिषासुर (mahiṣāsura) – buffalo demon; नृप (nṛpa) – king; अति (ati) – very; अभिमानी। (abhimānī) – proud; जेहि (jehi) – who; अघ (agha) – sin; भार (bhāra) – burden; मही (mahī) – earth; अकुलानी॥२२॥ (akulānī) – disturbed;

contentBlocks.original:
रूप कराल कालिका धारा।
सेन सहित तुम तिहि संहारा॥२३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rūpa karāla kālikā dhārā.
sena sahita tuma tihi saṃhārā..23..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 23: You assumed the terrifying form of Kālikā. Together with your army you destroyed him entirely.
contentBlocks.meanings: रूप (rūpa) – form; कराल (karāla) – fierce; कालिका (kālikā) – Kali; धारा। (dhārā) – stream; सेन (sena) – army; सहित (sahita) – together with; तुम (tum) – you; तिहि (tihi) – three; संहारा॥२३॥ (saṃhāra) – destroyed;

contentBlocks.original:
परी गाढ़ सन्तन पर जब जब।
भई सहाय मातु तुम तब तब॥२४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parī gār̤ha santana para jaba jaba.
bhaī sahāya mātu tuma taba taba..24..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 24: Whenever deep trouble has befallen the saints, you have become their helper and mother every time.
contentBlocks.meanings: परी (parī) – tested; गाढ़ (gāḍha) – intense; सन्तन (santana) – of saints; पर (para) – beyond; जब (jab) – when; जब। (jab.) – when; भई (bhaī) – became; सहाय (sahāya) – help; मातु (mātu) – mother; तुम (tum) – you; तब (tab) – then; तब॥२४॥ (tab॥24॥) – then;

contentBlocks.original:
अमरपुरी अरु बासव लोका।
तब महिमा सब रहें अशोका॥२५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amarapurī aru bāsava lokā.
taba mahimā saba raheṃ aśokā..25..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 25: In Amarāvatī (the city of the gods) and in Indra's realm — all dwell in grief-free joy through your glory.
contentBlocks.meanings: अमरपुरी (amarapurī) – immortal city (अमर:immortal | पुरी:city); अरु (aru) – and; बासव (bāsava) – of Indra; लोका। (lokā) – worlds; तब (taba) – then; महिमा (mahimā) – glory; सब (sab) – all; रहें (rahēṁ) – remain; अशोका॥२५॥ (aśokā) – without sorrow;

contentBlocks.original:
ज्वाला में है ज्योति तुम्हारी।
तुम्हें सदा पूजें नर-नारी॥२६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jvālā meṃ hai jyoti tumhārī.
tumheṃ sadā pūjeṃ nara-nārī..26..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 26: In the Jvālāmukhī shrine your sacred fire burns. Men and women worship you always.
contentBlocks.meanings: ज्वाला (jvālā) – flame; में (meṃ) – in; है (hai) – is; ज्योति (jyoti) – light; तुम्हारी। (tumhārī) – your; तुम्हें (tumheṃ) – you; सदा (sadā) – always; पूजें (pūjeṃ) – worship; नर-नारी॥२६॥ (nara-nārī) – men and women (नर:men | नारी:women);

contentBlocks.original:
प्रेम भक्ति से जो यश गावें।
दुःख दारिद्र निकट नहिं आवें॥२७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prema bhakti se jo yaśa gāveṃ.
duḥkha dāridra nikaṭa nahiṃ āveṃ..27..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 27: Those who sing your glory with love and devotion — sorrow and poverty do not come near them.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रेम (prema) – love; भक्ति (bhakti) – devotion; से (se) – with; जो (jo) – who; यश (yaśa) – fame; गावें। (gāveṁ) – sing; दुःख (duḥkha) – sorrow; दारिद्र (dāridra) – poverty; निकट (nikaṭa) – near; नहिं (nahiṁ) – not; आवें॥२७॥ (āveṁ) – come;

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यावे तुम्हें जो नर मन लाई।
जन्म-मरण ताकौ छुटि जाई॥२८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāve tumheṃ jo nara mana lāī.
janma-maraṇa tākau chuṭi jāī..28..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 28: The person who meditates upon you with wholehearted attention — the cycle of birth and death releases its hold on them.
contentBlocks.meanings: ध्यावे (dhyāve) – meditates; तुम्हें (tumheṁ) – you; जो (jo) – who; नर (nara) – man; मन (mana) – mind; लाई। (lāī) – brings; जन्म-मरण (janma-maraṇa) – birth and death (जन्म:birth | मरण:death); ताकौ (tākau) – his; छुटि (chuṭi) – freedom; जाई॥२८॥ (jāī) – goes;

contentBlocks.original:
जोगी सुर मुनि कहत पुकारी।
योग न हो बिन शक्ति तुम्हारी॥२९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jogī sura muni kahata pukārī.
yoga na ho bina śakti tumhārī..29..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 29: Yogis, gods, and sages cry out: 'Without your Śakti, no yoga is possible!'
contentBlocks.meanings: जोगी (jogī) – yogi; सुर (sura) – god; मुनि (muni) – sage; कहत (kahat) – say; पुकारी। (pukārī) – call out; योग (yoga) – yoga; न (na) – not; हो (ho) – be; बिन (bin) – without; शक्ति (śakti) – power; तुम्हारी॥२९॥ (tumhārī) – your;

contentBlocks.original:
शंकर आचारज तप कीनो।
काम अरु क्रोध जीति सब लीनो॥३०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṃkara ācāraja tapa kīno.
kāma aru krodha jīti saba līno..30..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 30: Śaṅkarācārya performed great austerities. He conquered lust and anger completely.
contentBlocks.meanings: शंकर (śaṅkara) – Shankara; आचारज (ācāraja) – teacher; तप (tapa) – austerity; कीनो। (kīno) – performed; काम (kāma) – desire; अरु (aru) – and; क्रोध (krodha) – anger; जीति (jīti) – conquered; सब (sab) – all; लीनो॥३०॥ (līno) – absorbed;

contentBlocks.original:
निशिदिन ध्यान धरो शंकर को।
काहु काल नहिं सुमिरो तुमको॥३१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
niśidina dhyāna dharo śaṃkara ko.
kāhu kāla nahiṃ sumiro tumako..31..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 31: Day and night he meditated on Śaṅkara (Śiva). At no time did he think of you (the Goddess).
contentBlocks.meanings: निशिदिन (niśidina) – day and night; ध्यान (dhyāna) – meditation; धरो (dharo) – hold; शंकर (śaṅkara) – Shankara; को। (ko) – whom; काहु (kāhu) – anyone; काल (kāla) – time; नहिं (nahiṁ) – not; सुमिरो (sumiro) – remember; तुमको॥३१॥ (tumako) – you;

contentBlocks.original:
शक्ति रूप का मरम न पायो।
शक्ति गई तब मन पछितायो॥३२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śakti rūpa kā marama na pāyo.
śakti gaī taba mana pachitāyo..32..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 32: He had not grasped the mystery of Śakti's form. When his power departed, only then did he repent.
contentBlocks.meanings: शक्ति (śakti) – power; रूप (rūpa) – form; का (kā) – of; मरम (maram) – essence; न (na) – not; पायो। (pāyo) – obtained; शक्ति (śakti) – power; गई (gaī) – gone; तब (tab) – then; मन (man) – mind; पछितायो॥३२॥ (pachitāyo) – regretted;

contentBlocks.original:
शरणागत हुई कीर्ति बखानी।
जय जय जय जगदम्ब भवानी॥३३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaraṇāgata huī kīrti bakhānī.
jaya jaya jaya jagadamba bhavānī..33..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 33: Having taken refuge, his praise was proclaimed: 'Victory, victory, victory to Jagadambā Bhavānī!'
contentBlocks.meanings: शरणागत (śaraṇāgata) – those who seek refuge (शरण:refuge | आगत:come); हुई (huī) – became; कीर्ति (kīrti) – fame; बखानी। (bakhānī) – described; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; जय (jaya) – victory; जगदम्ब (jagadamba) – Mother of the world (जगत्:world | अम्ब:mother); भवानी॥३३॥ (bhavānī) – Goddess Bhavani;

contentBlocks.original:
भई प्रसन्न आदि जगदम्बा।
दई शक्ति नहिं कीन विलम्बा॥३४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaī prasanna ādi jagadambā.
daī śakti nahiṃ kīna vilambā..34..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 34: The primordial Jagadambā was pleased. She restored his power without delay.
contentBlocks.meanings: भई (bhaī) – became; प्रसन्न (prasanna) – pleased; आदि (ādi) – primordial; जगदम्बा। (jagadambā) – Mother of the world; दई (daī) – gave; शक्ति (śakti) – power; नहिं (nahiṁ) – not; कीन (kīna) – made; विलम्बा॥३४॥ (vilambā) – delay;

contentBlocks.original:
मोको मातु कष्ट अति घेरो।
तुम बिन कौन हरै दुःख मेरो॥३५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
moko mātu kaṣṭa ati ghero.
tuma bina kauna harai duḥkha mero..35..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 35: I am surrounded, O Mother, by overwhelming suffering. Without you, who can remove my sorrow?
contentBlocks.meanings: मोको (moko) – to me; मातु (mātu) – mother; कष्ट (kaṣṭa) – trouble; अति (ati) – very; घेरो। (ghero) – surrounds; तुम (tum) – you; बिन (bin) – without; कौन (kaun) – who; हरै (harai) – removes; दुःख (duḥkha) – sorrow; मेरो॥३५॥ (mero) – my;

contentBlocks.original:
आशा तृष्णा निपट सतावें।
मोह मदादिक सब बिनशावें॥३६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āśā tṛṣṇā nipaṭa satāveṃ.
moha madādika saba binaśāveṃ..36..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 36: Hope and thirst torment me utterly. May delusion, pride, and all such vices be destroyed.
contentBlocks.meanings: आशा (āśā) – hope; तृष्णा (tṛṣṇā) – desire; निपट (nipaṭa) – completely; सतावें। (satāveṁ) – torment; मोह (moha) – delusion; मदादिक (madādika) – pride and others (मद:pride | आदिक:and others); सब (sab) – all; बिनशावें॥३६॥ (binaśāveṁ) – destroy;

contentBlocks.original:
शत्रु नाश कीजै महारानी।
सुमिरौं इकचित तुम्हें भवानी॥३७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śatru nāśa kījai mahārānī.
sumirauṃ ikacita tumheṃ bhavānī..37..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 37: Destroy my enemies, O Great Queen. I remember you with single-pointed mind, O Bhavānī.
contentBlocks.meanings: शत्रु (śatru) – enemy; नाश (nāśa) – destruction; कीजै (kījai) – do; महारानी। (mahārāṇī) – great queen; सुमिरौं (sumirauṁ) – I remember; इकचित (ikacit) – with concentration; तुम्हें (tumheṁ) – you; भवानी॥३७॥ (bhavānī) – Goddess Bhavani;

contentBlocks.original:
करो कृपा हे मातु दयाला।
ऋद्धि-सिद्धि दै करहु निहाला॥३८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karo kṛpā he mātu dayālā.
ṛddhi-siddhi dai karahu nihālā..38..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 38: Show your grace, O compassionate Mother. Grant Ṛddhi (prosperity) and Siddhi (perfection) and make me content.
contentBlocks.meanings: करो (karo) – do; कृपा (kṛpā) – compassion; हे (he) – O; मातु (mātu) – mother; दयाला। (dayālā) – compassionate; ऋद्धि-सिद्धि (ṛddhi-siddhi) – prosperity and success (ऋद्धि:prosperity | सिद्धि:success); दै (dai) – give; करहु (karahu) – make; निहाला।३८॥ (nihālā) – happy;

contentBlocks.original:
जब लगि जिऊँ दया फल पाऊँ।
तुम्हरो यश मैं सदा सुनाऊँ॥३९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaba lagi jiūm̐ dayā phala pāūm̐.
tumharo yaśa maiṃ sadā sunāūm̐..39..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 39: For as long as I live, may I receive the fruit of your grace. I shall always spread your glory.
contentBlocks.meanings: जब (jab) – when; लगि (lagi) – until; जिऊँ (jiūṁ) – live; दया (dayā) – compassion; फल (phala) – fruit; पाऊँ। (pāūṁ) – receive; तुम्हरो (tumharo) – your; यश (yaśa) – glory; मैं (maiṁ) – I; सदा (sadā) – always; सुनाऊँ॥३९॥ (sunāūṁ) – recite;

contentBlocks.original:
श्री दुर्गा चालीसा जो कोई गावै।
सब सुख भोग परमपद पावै॥४०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī durgā cālīsā jo koī gāvai.
saba sukha bhoga paramapada pāvai..40..
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 40 — Phalaśruti: Whoever sings this Śrī Durgā Cālīsā enjoys all pleasures and attains the supreme state (paramapada).
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; दुर्गा (durgā) – Durga; चालीसा (cālīsā) – forty verses; जो (yo) – who; कोई (ko'i) – anyone; गावै। (gāvai) – sings; सब (sab) – all; सुख (sukha) – happiness; भोग (bhoga) – enjoyment; परमपद (paramapada) – supreme state (परम:supreme | पद:state); पावै॥४०॥ (pāvai) – attains;

contentBlocks.original:
देवीदास शरण निज जानी।
करहु कृपा जगदम्ब भवानी॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
devīdāsa śaraṇa nija jānī.
karahu kṛpā jagadamba bhavānī..
contentBlocks.translation: Closing verse by Devīdāsa: Devīdāsa, knowing himself to be your refuge, prays: Show your grace, O Jagadambā Bhavānī!
contentBlocks.meanings: देवीदास (devīdāsa) – servant of the goddess (देवी:goddess | दास:servant); शरण (śaraṇa) – refuge; निज (nija) – own; जानी। (jānī) – know; करहु (karahu) – do; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; जगदम्ब (jagadamba) – mother of the world (जगत्:world | अम्ब:mother); भवानी॥ (bhavānī) – Bhavani;
